meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Debugging with AI to Solve Slow Development
content.seo_description: Stop wasting hours on bugs. Learn how to debug with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to reclaim your development speed as a professional freelancer.
content.answer_block: To eliminate slow development cycles, integrate AI agents directly into your terminal. By pairing Antigravity for architectural oversight with Claude Code for iterative debugging, you automate stack traces and logic errors. This workflow reduces debugging time by 70%, allowing you to ship client projects with production-grade reliability.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is pinging you on Slack, and you are staring at a cryptic stack trace that makes zero sense. We have all been there—lost in the weeds of a refactor that should have taken an hour but has consumed your entire afternoon. Slow development isn't just a technical hurdle; it is a direct hit to your bottom line and your reputation as a reliable freelancer.

But the era of manual debugging is dead. By leveraging AI-native tooling, you can transform your terminal into a partner that doesn't just suggest fixes, but executes them with surgical precision. It is time to stop guessing and start building with the speed that high-paying clients expect.

steps.title: Establish the Debugging Context
steps.description: Before throwing code at an agent, define the scope of the error to avoid hallucinations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided stack trace and identify the root cause within the authentication service. Focus on potential race conditions during the token refresh cycle.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze auth_service.ts for race conditions'

steps.title: Isolate the Faulty Module
steps.description: Use AI to prune unnecessary noise and focus on the specific file causing the runtime failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal reproduction script for the current error state in isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create reproduction script for auth failure'

steps.title: Automated Patch Generation
steps.description: Generate a fix that adheres to your existing project architecture and style guidelines.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a fix for the identified bug that strictly follows our existing TypeScript interfaces and error handling patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply fix to auth_service.ts with type safety'

steps.title: Verify Through Unit Testing
steps.description: Don't trust the fix until the AI writes the test case to prove the failure is resolved.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test suite that triggers the previous error and asserts the new successful state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'write jest test for auth_service.ts success path'

steps.title: Final Integration and Clean-up
steps.description: Commit the changes and document the fix for future maintainability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the bug fix and generate a clear commit message adhering to conventional commits standard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'git commit -m "fix: resolve race condition in auth refresh"'

pro_tips: Always force the AI to explain its reasoning before proposing code to prevent 'lazy' patches.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to keep track of project-specific architectural constraints across sessions.
When debugging complex state, ask Antigravity to visualize the state tree before applying any logic changes.
Keep your context window clean by deleting temporary reproduction files immediately after the test passes.
Set up a custom 'system prompt' in your terminal environment that mandates defensive coding practices for all AI-generated patches.
content.next_steps: Once your debugging workflow is automated, focus on building a library of 'Golden Prompts' that handle your most frequent client requests, effectively creating your own internal coding framework.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs dictate your deadlines. By mastering AI-assisted debugging, you aren't just writing code faster—you are delivering better value to your clients and reclaiming your free time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
